Job_Title: Microsoft 365 Engineer – Identity & Core Infrastructure Operations
Company: Central Retail Corporation Public Company Limited
Full_Description:
Job Type: Full-time | Location: Bangkok | Experience: 2–5 Years

About the Role

We're looking for an M365 Engineer to join our Core Infrastructure team — someone who's genuinely hands-on with Microsoft identity and collaboration services, not just familiar with the names.

You'll be working in a complex enterprise environment with multiple business units, hybrid identity setup, and a full Microsoft 365 stack. Small team, real ownership, actual impact.

Key Responsibilities

Manage and operate Active Directory (AD DS) and Microsoft Entra ID in a hybrid environment

Administer and troubleshoot AD Connect / Entra Connect — sync issues, object errors, configuration changes

Administer Microsoft 365 services — Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive for Business

Manage user lifecycle — provisioning, licensing, group policy, and access governance

Configure and monitor Conditional Access, MFA policies, and compliance settings

Troubleshoot identity and collaboration issues with proper root-cause analysis

Collaborate with Security, Network, and Application teams on cross-functional projects

Create and maintain documentation — runbooks, configuration records, change logs

Requirements

2–5 years of hands-on experience with Active Directory in a production environment

Solid understanding of Microsoft 365 Admin Center and at least 2 of the following: Exchange Online / SharePoint Online / Teams Admin / Entra ID

Working knowledge of GPO, OU design, and RBAC

Understanding of Hybrid Identity concepts (AD Connect / Entra Connect)

Ability to troubleshoot independently — not just follow runbooks

Good communication skills — this team works cross-functionally

Nice to Have

Microsoft Certification: MS-102, AZ-800, or SC-300

Experience with Conditional Access, DLP, or Microsoft Purview

Basic PowerShell scripting for automation

Exposure to AD security hardening or security assessment

What You'll Get

Work in a real enterprise environment — multi-BU, hybrid setup, high complexity

Small, focused team with direct ownership over infrastructure decisions

Clear growth path toward cloud identity and Entra ID specialization

Structured team with a lead who actually develops the team

Why This Role Stands Out

This is not a helpdesk role in disguise. If you've been doing real infrastructure work and want an environment where your skills actually matter — this is worth your time.

Job_Title: Data Center Technician III, AWS Infrastructure Operations
Full_Description:
Data Center Operations designs, installs & maintains the world’s largest Cloud Computing Infrastructure.

We are looking for skilled Data Center Linux & Hardware Engineers with a passion for technology to help us expand our Cloud to the next level.
Amazon Web Services (AWS) offers an exciting, dynamic and challenging environment encouraging creativity and personal development while maintaining AWS computing environments in a secure, scalable, and cost-effective manner.
We continue to expand our Data Centers in every region. This requires talented people to build & manage. We hope it is you!

At Amazon, career progression is part of our environment. We want you to progress. If your career path is in Systems, Network or Database Engineering, Software Development or maybe AWS Support, Technical Operations or perhaps Project Management, we will create a development plan to enable you to succeed reaching those goals. This begins on Day One!
Come and work for the world’s most Customer Centric Company.

The Opportunity: Data Center Linux & Hardware Engineer

This role is a unique opportunity to work in some of the most cutting edge data centers in the world. Amazon data centers are large-scale high-density centers where you will be working on changing the face of Cloud technology in the region.

A Data Center Linux & Hardware Engineer may be the primary point of contact for both internal customers (for example: Network Engineers, Systems Engineers, Software Developers, Database Engineers, Technical Operations) and external customers (Hardware Vendors, Contractors, Service Providers among others).

There is never a dull moment as each day presents itself with different challenges. Some of the key responsibilities you will undertake are:

Problem Solving:
- Maintain a high level of system reliability by prioritizing and resolving trouble tickets efficiently, these include:
- Escalation point and technical troubleshooter for all Systems and Network hardware problems
- Deep diving into Linux server issues
- XEN service virtualization troubleshooting

Technical:
- Troubleshoot technical issues on various platforms ranging from Systems through Networking to Power/Mechanical
- Remediation of physical layer outages, both

Systems & Network
- Remediation or recovery of physical power issues on racks
- Participate in Data Center power & cooling events
Operations:
- Meet 24x7 On-Call requirements and response during shift rotations.
- Install & configure racks of hosts in line with internal SLAs
- Triage & resolve trouble tickets for all devices in your region
- Data Center point of contact for all High Severity issues
- Physical replacement of server and network device parts
- Ensure correct rotation of parts & spares
- Help define metrics to increase our customer uptime
- Enforcing Amazons Security Best Practices
- Interact with third party vendors & contractors
- Contribute ideas to improve operational efficiency
- Engage with Remote Hands & Eyes in EU Regional Cloudfront POPs

Project Management:
- Participate in and deliver on a number of high impact small to mid-scale projects
- Participate in team meetings for metric analysis and project status updates
- Help build the world’s largest Cloud infrastructure

Mentoring:
[ol] - Share knowledge and help educate less technical staff on the best practices related to all service owner issues

Hiring:
- Contribute towards building a great team by getting involved in the Amazon hiring process/candidate interviews

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Job_Title: Senior Data Engineer (Cloud Data platforms Azure, AWS)

Full_Description:
Job Overview:

Sri Trang Group is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior Data Engineer to join our dynamic engineering team. As a Senior Data Engineer, you will play a critical role in managing and optimizing our data infrastructure, ensuring seamless integration of data from various sources, including Azure, AWS, SAP, MySQL Server, and machine data. Your expertise will be instrumental in supporting our AI, data analytics, and digital transformation initiatives. 

Key Responsibilities:

Data Pipeline Development: Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines to ingest, process, and store data from multiple sources, including Azure, AWS, SAP, MySQL Server, and machine data.

Data Integration: Develop and implement processes for integrating data from disparate sources, ensuring seamless data flow and consistency across systems and platforms.

Data Quality and Governance: Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and security by implementing data quality and governance best practices. Collaborate with the Data Governance team to enforce these practices.

Collaboration: Work closely with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions that support analytics and AI initiatives.

Performance Optimization: Monitor and optimize the performance of data infrastructure to ensure efficiency and scalability. Identify and resolve bottlenecks and performance issues.

Technology Evaluation: Stay current with emerging data engineering technologies and trends. Evaluate and recommend new tools and technologies that can improve data processes and infrastructure.

Documentation and Training: Develop comprehensive documentation for data engineering processes and systems. Provide training and support to team members and stakeholders on data tools and best practices.

Project Leadership: Lead and manage data engineering projects, ensuring they are delivered on time, within scope, and meet quality standards.

Education:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field. A Master’s degree is preferred but not required.

Qualifications:

Experience: At least 5 years of experience in data engineering, with a proven track record of designing and managing large-scale data infrastructure. Experience in cloud data platforms (Azure, AWS) is highly desirable.

Technical Skills: Proficiency in data engineering tools and technologies such as SQL, Python, Spark, Hadoop, Kafka, and ETL processes. Strong knowledge of data warehousing, data modeling, and big data technologies.

Expertise in integrating data from multiple sources, including Azure, AWS, SAP, MySQL Server, and machine data.

Analytical Skills: Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to analyze complex data requirements and develop effective solutions.

Detail-Oriented: Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and integrity.

Strong knowledge of data manipulation and visualization tools.

Proficiency in Power BI and Tableau (SF) for data visualization and reporting.

Working knowledge and expertise in various cybersecurity technology

Report to: Chief Digital Officer

Position Overview

In collaboration with the Chief Digital Officer, the Digital Support Manager plays a critical role in managing the support, event coordination, project management, and operations of the Digital department. This position involves supervising Digital Specialists, overseeing support operations, and ensuring the efficient functioning of Digital infrastructure.

The Digital Support Manager aligns digital strategies with educational goals and ensures adherence to policies and procedures. The role demands a strong focus on supporting educational systems, collaborating with the academic team, and handling high-level stakeholder management.

Academic & Professional Qualifications 

Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field.

Strong knowledge of current network and computer systems, hardware, protocols, and standards.

Demonstrated capacity to promote and encourage technology integration in education and daily business operations.

Excellent written and oral communication skills in Thai and English.

Skills, Experience, & Attributes Sought 

Proven leadership skills with experience in managing Digital operations.

Excellent communication skills in Thai and English.

Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

Familiarity with basic Digital operational procedures.

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to troubleshoot technical issues.

Experience in ticketing systems and assigning tickets.

Duties and Responsibilities

Leadership & Operations Management

Team Supervision: Supervise, train, and mentor assigned staff to be responsible for providing technical support.

Helpdesk Operations: Ensure the efficient functioning of Digital helpdesk operations, addressing user queries, assigning tickets, and providing technical assistance.

KPI Tracking: Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and service level agreements (SLAs), ensuring timely resolution of support requests.

Complaint Resolution: Act as the primary point of contact for escalated user complaints and dispute resolution, managing stakeholder expectations professionally.

Network & Systems Collaboration: Collaborate closely with the Network and Systems teams to ensure infrastructure stability. Note: This role does not supervise Network and Systems staff but is expected to coordinate workflows effectively with them.

Escalation: Escalate complex issues to Digital administrators and management members as needed.

Collaborate with Digital Support Managers at other campuses to share best practices and ensure consistent user experiences across the group.

Liaise with the District Digital team to align campus operations with group-wide technology standards.

Event Coordination & Project Management

Event Support: Lead the planning and deployment of Digital resources for school events, including staffing allocation and on-site technical support. This includes managing after-hours and weekend support schedules as required.

Project Management: Lead and manage Digital projects from initiation to closure. This includes defining scope, scheduling, resource planning, and ensuring projects are delivered on time.

Resource Planning: Collaborate with the Digital Leadership team to develop and implement Digital strategies aligned with educational goals.

Educational Systems & Academic Collaboration

Academic Liaison: Collaborate directly with the Academics team to understand their technology needs, providing assistance and strategic advice where necessary.

Educational Platforms: Manage and provide support for educational subscriptions as required.

Training: Provide routine training to staff on best practices for Digital tools and educational software.

Integration: Promote and encourage technology integration in education and daily business operations.


Asset & Change Management

Asset Tracking: Ensure systems are in place to manage and maintain all infrastructure assets, including documentation, tagging, and tracking.

Auditing: Perform periodic asset audits to ensure accurate asset records.

Disposal: Submit and verify an accurate asset disposal list for assets no longer in use.

Licensing: Ensure proper licensing for all hardware and assigned systems.

Upgrades: Plan for infrastructure and maintenance upgrades in consultation with the Line Manager.




Policy Compliance, Information Security & Reporting

Policy Adherence: Ensure Digital staff follow information security protocols according to defined policies.

Data Protection: Enforce and monitor the school’s approach to GDPR and PDPA, providing training and guidance to mitigate data breaches.

Change Control: Operate an effective change management procedure, ensuring policy compliance, and report any breaches to the Line Manager.

Security Reporting: Report any observed security vulnerabilities or potential risks to Digital management promptly.

Operational Reporting: Provide regular updates to the Digital team on support activities, ticket resolutions, and identify trends and patterns in support requests.

Personal Development

Maintain an up-to-date IT / digital certification.

Stay abreast of the latest digital technology through workshops and training sessions.

Additional Responsibilities

Perform additional duties and responsibilities as assigned.

KISRP International School is committed to creating a safe and supportive environment in which our students can feel secure in all of their experiences within the school community. All members of the community should at all times show respect and understanding for an individual’s rights, safety, and welfare, and understand that all play a role in the safeguarding of our students, conducting themselves in a way that follows the law and reflects the ethos and values of the school. 

KISRP uses the comprehensive international standards developed by the International Taskforce for Child Protection as this provides a framework for the implementation of safeguarding policies and procedures. Our school's child safeguarding policy and supporting policies and procedures are written in support of the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child and aim to follow procedures stated by the Thai Ministry of Education, Thai Employment Law, and the Thai Child Protection Policy.

Our Corporate Risk Office of Charoen Pokphand Group Co., Ltd. plays an integral part in helping the group companies within Charoen Pokphand Group to achieve the entities’ objectives and to manage performance, optimize risk exposure in financial, operation, and minimize non-compliance and business mishap.

We’re looking for an outside-the-box thinker who enjoys working as a team to help us shaping our organization’s future. We work shoulder-to-shoulder with the group companies to solve real world complex business problems and to fundamentally improve and grow the business in the sustainable manner. Our team comprises of experienced professionals in process improvement, risk management, accounting, intelligence, technology, internal controls, data analytics and deep industry skills.

Responsibilities

The specific responsibilities of this role include:

Develop and implement cyber risk management strategy as a group level that guides and informs company risk decisions (including how risk is framed, assessed, responded to, and monitored over time.

Follow strategic alignment of cyber risk management decisions with missions and business functions consistent with company goals and objectives.

Prepare cyber risk management program to develop plan that will help to decrease cyber risk factors.

Develop, establish, and implement cyber risk management policies, frameworks, guideline includes risk appetite, risk tolerance, and risk matrix for cyber risk management.

Derive the group’s cyber risk management standard and implement it throughout company.

Provide oversight for cyber risk management activities carried out by company to ensure consistent and effective risk-based decisions.

Perform cyber risk evaluation, which entails comparing potential risk with company-defined criteria such as cost, environmental issues, and regulatory requirements, and evaluating the company’s previous risk management.

Perform cyber risk assessment results and provide recommendation to business.

Design and create cyber risk assessment report and dashboard.

Being highly knowledgeable about the cyber security threatscape and assists teams and business units, provide consultation to all related cyber risk management.

Qualifications and Skills

Bachelor degree in Computer Science/Engineering, Information Technology, Information Systems, Accounting Information Systems or other related fields. Master’s degree is a plus.

Relevant experience in Cyber Security, IT/Cyber risk management, with minimum of 4 years in the design and implementation of IT/Cyber risk management program.

Extensive experience with the design, development, and implementation of IT/cyber risk assessment.

Knowledge in Cyber Security best practice and Risk analysis

Working knowledge and expertise in various cybersecurity technology

Good written and verbal communication skills with other risk management professionals to properly appreciate the company’s key risks and create effective risk management solutions.

Experience working in various industries, including finance and banking. As a result, an IT/Cyber risk manager should have a thorough understanding of computer systems.

Experienced in Cyber risk assessment program is advantage.

Hold professional certifications e.g. ISC2, GIAC, ISACA, EC-Council or other security certifications is preferred.

Familiar with ISO/IEC 27000 series and frameworks such as COBIT, OSSTMM, ITIL, NIST and OWASP.

RMA Group, headquartered in Bangkok, Thailand, is a multi-sector group with over 8000 employees and an operational presence in 14 countries. For over 30 years, we have developed enduring partnerships to support the needs of our clients, underpinned by the core values of Innovation, Integrity and Endurance. Through its various businesses included RMA Automotive, Global Fleet Sales, Comin Asia and Express Food Group (EFG), RMA Group has established itself as the partner of choice for leading automotive, equipment, engineering services, and food brands in Southeast Asia and beyond.

Overview Roles:

The ERP Business Process & Support is responsible for managing, improving, and optimizing ERP-related business functions across departments. This role serves as a key bridge between business users and the ITM technical team, ensuring that ERP systems effectively support day-to-day operations, data accuracy, and compliance standards throughout sales, product development, production, supply chain, finance processes, aftersales services and strategic business goals.

The position oversees functional support, process optimization, training, data accuracy, and the coordination of business requirements for system enhancements, driving continuous improvement and successful system adoption across the organization.

Key Responsibilities

1. Business Process Management & Optimization

Lead the review, documentation, and improvement of ERP-enabled business processes.

Work with process owners to identify inefficiencies and implement ERP-driven solutions.

Ensure business processes are standardized and aligned with corporate policies.

2. ERP Functional Support

·       Coordinate with the ITM and Product Development teams to facilitate product releases and ensure seamless data integration between the ERP and PLM systems.

Provide first-line functional support and troubleshooting for ERP users.

Coordinate with ITM and vendors for timely issue resolution, maintain user roles, permissions, and master data accuracy.

Develop and maintain training materials and conduct user training sessions.

3. Change Management & System Enhancement Support

Participate in testing and validation of system enhancements or configuration changes.

Support change control procedures and communicate updates to relevant stakeholders.

Ensure smooth user adoption through training, communication, and follow-up.

4. Reporting & Data Quality

Develop and maintain standard reports and dashboards to support business monitoring.

Ensure data accuracy and perform periodic data audits.

Collaborate and coordinate with teams to enhance data governance, improve reporting efficiency, and maintain data consistency across systems and departments.

Qualifications

Education:

Bachelor’s Degree or higher in Information Technology Systems, Business Administration, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Business Management or other related fields.

ERP functional training or certification e.g., Oracle NetSuite, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics (Oracle NetSuite preferred).

Experience:

3–7 years of experience in ERP solution design, ERP functional support, process improvement, data management or business operations.

Experience with cross-functional business processes in automotive or manufacturing operations, with strong understanding of sales, CRM, financial & accounting, product development, procurement, production, inventory control and after-sales.

Experience with ERP integration, scripting, or reporting tools is an advantage. (e.g., Siemens PLM Teamcenter, EDI, Power BI)

Experience supporting Oracle NetSuite ERP users or similar ERP systems.

Skills & Competencies:

Strong understanding of system architecture, process mapping, end-to-end workflows and automotive business processes

Excellent problem-solving and communication skills.

Ability to translate business needs into actionable ERP improvements.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.

Ability to translate user needs into actionable system or process improvements, while facilitating cross-functional collaboration and driving continuous improvement.

Good command in English

Able to travel both domestic and international.

Key Success Metrics

High ERP user satisfaction and timely issue resolution.

Reduction in manual or redundant business processes.

Ensure consistent and accurate ERP master data, transactional integrity, and reporting quality, while continuously improving process efficiency and data governance.

Effective training delivery and user enablement.

Effective user enablement and successful adoption of ERP system changes and new functionalities.

Scope of work:

The Sustainability Data Specialist will be responsible for the end-to-end management of our sustainability data, from designing and maintaining the core database to collecting, analyzing, and visualizing ESG metrics. This role is essential for supporting our strategic decision-making and external reporting through the development of data-driven tools, advanced analytics, and clear, compelling reports and dashboards, while also promoting innovation in data management best practices.




Responsibilities:

Database Management: Design, develop, and maintain a robust sustainability database to store and manage all environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics.

Data Collection & Integration: Collect and integrate ESG data from internal sources to support group-wide sustainability strategy, reporting, and performance tracking aligned with recognized frameworks.

Analytical Tools & Techniques: Analyze complex sustainability datasets using database querying, data validation, and advanced analytics to generate actionable insights.

Data Tools & Support: Develop user-friendly data input tools for internal teams and provide the necessary training and assistance to ensure accurate data collection that drives strategic goals.

Reporting & Visualization: Develop compelling dashboards and reports that translate complex data into clear, actionable visuals for both internal strategic decision-making and external reporting.

Innovation & Best Practices: Research and champion new technologies and industry best practices in sustainability data management.




Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or higher in Data Science, Computer Science, Environmental Science, or a related field.

2-3 years of professional experience in data management, business intelligence, or a similar role.

Proficiency in data visualization tools like Power BI or Tableau.

Experience using a programming language, preferably Python, for data analysis, data querying, and AI/ML applications.

Prior experience with sustainability or ESG data, and a solid understanding of key reporting frameworks (e.g., GRI, SASB).

English language proficiency (TOEIC > 750) .

Strong communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate with diverse teams.

ปรับใช้มาตรฐาน นโยบายและ Improvement กระบวนการต่างๆ ที่เกี่ยวข้องกับรูปแบบ โครงสร้างข้อมูล/BI ของสายธุรกิจให้พร้อมใช้งานอย่างมีประสิทธิภาพ

พัฒนาแนวทางและเกณฑ์การทดสอบระบบ BI พร้อมทั้งออกแบบหลักสูตรอบรมและโค้ชชิ่งการใช้งานเครื่องมือ BI สำหรับผู้ใช้งาน เพื่อเพิ่มศักยภาพในการตัดสินใจเชิงข้อมูล

🎯 WE ARE HIRING! | DevSecOps Engineer




Job Purpose

มีหน้าที่รับผิดชอบในการออกแบบ ติดตั้ง และบริหาร DevSecOps Platform ขององค์กรบน AWS เป็นหลัก โดยบูรณาการ Security เข้าสู่ทุกขั้นตอนของ SDLC ผ่าน CI/CD Pipeline อัตโนมัติ และบริหาร Cloud Infrastructure ด้วยหลักการ IaC เพื่อให้ทีมสามารถส่งมอบซอฟต์แวร์ที่ปลอดภัย มีคุณภาพ และ Scale ได้อย่างต่อเนื่อง

Key Responsibilities

1.  กลยุทธ์และการนำองค์กร (Strategy & Leadership)

•    กำหนดกลยุทธ์และ Roadmap ด้าน DevSecOps ขององค์กร ให้สอดคล้องกับเป้าหมายธุรกิจและมาตรฐานความปลอดภัย

•    นำทีม DevSecOps ในการออกแบบและดูแล Kubernetes Cluster (Amazon EKS), API Gateway และ Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) บน AWS เป็นหลัก

•    ประเมินและคัดเลือก Security Tools, Cloud Services และ Third-party Solutions ที่เหมาะสมกับองค์กร




2.  ความปลอดภัยและ CI/CD Pipeline (Security & Pipeline)

•    บูรณาการ Security Gates และ Automated Security Testing (SAST, DAST, SCA, Container Image Scanning) เข้ากับ CI/CD Pipeline เพื่อให้ทุก Release ผ่านการตรวจสอบโดยอัตโนมัติ

•      บริหารจัดการ Secrets และ Credentials อย่างปลอดภัยด้วย HashiCorp Vault และ AWS Secrets Manager

•      ดูแลให้ระบบและกระบวนการพัฒนาสอดคล้องกับมาตรฐาน OWASP Top 10 และ CIS Benchmarks




3.  Cloud Infrastructure & Monitoring

•    ออกแบบและดูแล Cloud Infrastructure บน AWS เป็นหลัก และสามารถขยายไปยัง Huawei Cloud, GCP หรือ Azure ได้เมื่อจำเป็น

•      ออกแบบและดูแล Monitoring, Logging และ Alerting Stack (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK) เพื่อตรวจจับ Security Anomaly แบบ Real-time

•    สนับสนุน Continuous Delivery โดยดูแล Pipeline ให้มีเสถียรภาพ รวดเร็ว และปลอดภัยตลอดวงจรการพัฒนา




4.  การทำงานร่วมกับทีม (Collaboration & Agile)

•      ประสานงานกับทีม Agile/Scrum เพื่อผนวก Security Tasks เข้ากับ Sprint Planning และกำหนดเป้าหมายด้าน Security ร่วมกับทีม Dev และ QA

•    จัด Security Training และ Knowledge Sharing ให้กับทีมพัฒนาอย่างสม่ำเสมอ อย่างน้อย 1 ครั้ง/เดือน




5.  Incident Response & Continuous Improvement

•    วิเคราะห์และบริหาร Incident Response พร้อมจัดทำ Post-Mortem Analysis เพื่อระบุ Root Cause และป้องกันการเกิดซ้ำ

•      นำการปรับปรุงกระบวนการ DevSecOps และ Agile อย่างต่อเนื่อง (Continuous Improvement)




Job Qualification

1.  การศึกษา (Education)

•    วุฒิการศึกษาระดับปริญญาตรีขึ้นไป สาขาวิทยาการคอมพิวเตอร์, วิศวกรรมซอฟต์แวร์, Cybersecurity หรือสาขาที่เกี่ยวข้อง

•    อายุไม่เกิน 30 ปี




2.  ประสบการณ์ (Experience)

•      ประสบการณ์อย่างน้อย 5 ปี ในตำแหน่ง DevSecOps Engineer หรือ DevOps Engineer

•    มีประสบการณ์นำทีมด้าน DevSecOps หรือ Infrastructure อย่างน้อย 1-2 ปี

•      มีความเชี่ยวชาญใน AWS และเทคโนโลยีที่ระบุใน Tools & Tech Stack

•    มี Certification ด้าน Cloud หรือ Security เช่น AWS Certified, CISSP, CEH จะได้รับการพิจารณาเป็นพิเศษ




🚀 หากคุณมองหาโอกาสในการเติบโตในสาย DevSecOps Engineer พร้อมกับการทำงานที่มีคุณค่า และทีมที่ให้ความร่วมมือและสนับสนุนกัน — สมัครเลยวันนี้! ทำงานจันทร์-ศุกร์ เวลา 09.00-18.00 น. ขอคนที่สนใจและพร้อมจะก้าวไปด้วยกันสมัครกันเข้ามานะคะ แล้วเจอกันค่ะ

Full_Description:
About UOB

United Overseas Bank Limited (UOB) is a leading bank in Asia with a global network of more than 500 branches and offices in 19 countries and territories in Asia Pacific, Europe and North America. In Asia, we operate through our head office in Singapore and banking subsidiaries in China, Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand, as well as branches and offices. Our history spans more than 80 years. Over this time, we have been guided by our values – Honorable, Enterprising, United and Committed. This means we always strive to do what is right, build for the future, work as one team and pursue long-term success. It is how we work, consistently, be it towards the company, our colleagues or our customers.

Job Description

Role Overview

We are seeking for Financial Information Management to strengthen and evolve the Bank’s financial and regulatory systems. The role partners closely with Finance, Risk, and Technology to ensure system stability, data integrity, and regulatory readiness, while delivering strategic finance change initiatives.

This position balances operational excellence with practical innovation and is ideal for candidates who enjoy improving critical platforms while working closely with stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities:

Operational Excellence

•     Take end to end ownership of a primary regulatory or financial platform, with responsibility for a limited number of closely related systems, ensuring stability, accuracy, and resilience.

•     Maintain data integrity and controls across regulatory reporting, risk weighted assets/regulatory capital, accounting, financial reporting, and performance management domains.

•     Lead incident and problem management, including root cause analysis and remediation, with a focus on reducing recurring issues and improving KPIs.

•     Establish and maintain clear documentation, data lineage, runbooks, and backup coverage to ensure continuity and operational resilience.

•     Oversee UAT, change management, and production releases in collaboration with Technology (SDLC, environments, access).

Strategic Change & Innovation

•     Contribute to the strategic roadmap for financial systems in partnership with Group stakeholders.

•     Lead impact assessment and delivery support for finance change initiatives, including regulatory and business driven changes.

•     Translate business and regulatory needs into clear BRDs/FRDs and practical solution designs.

•     Identify opportunities to simplify processes, improve automation, and enhance reporting efficiency.

•     Act as a key point of contact for audit, regulatory, and senior stakeholder discussions related to assigned systems.

•     Promote structured ways of working that support knowledge sharing, cross training, and sustainable operations.

Qualifications & Experience

•     Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field (Master’s/professional certification is a plus).

•     7–12 years of experience in financial systems, regulatory reporting, or finance/risk technology within a bank or financial institution.

•     Strong understanding of regulatory reporting, regulatory capital/RWA, financial accounting, and financial reporting.

•     Experience supporting or delivering Basel II/III or Basel 4 initiatives (impact assessment, delivery, or testing) is highly preferred.

•     Proven experience in business analysis and delivery: requirements, process mapping, functional/technical specs, testing/UAT.

•     Solid data capability: SQL, data analysis, data modelling; familiarity with ETL/ELT pipelines and SDLC tools (e.g., Jira/Confluence, version control).

•     Strong communication skills in English and Thai, able to work effectively with both technical and non technical stakeholders.

Key Competencies:

•     Ownership & Accountability — takes responsibility for outcomes on assigned platforms.

•     Analytical Rigor — solves complex issues in regulated environments.

•     Influence & Collaboration — works effectively across Finance, Risk, and Technology.

•     Documentation & Controls — builds durable processes and reduces dependency risk through continuity practices.

•     Delivery Under Pressure — meets timelines with clear trade offs and structured governance.

Job_Title: Data Loss Prevention Officer (IT Risk Officer)
Full_Description:
Responsibilities:

Lead the development, implementation, and maintenance of data loss prevention rule policies, monitoring processes and procedures, and best practices to safeguard sensitive personal and company data.

Continuously review and update data loss prevention rule policies in response to emerging threats and evolving business needs.

Work closely with relevant Business Units to screen Whitelist processes and coordinate with the IT Security team to implement DLP rule policies configuration in the DLP system.

Lead investigations into data loss incidents, determine root causes, and recommend corrective actions to mitigate future risks.

Work closely with incident response teams to manage and resolve data security incidents, ensuring timely documentation and reporting.

Conduct regular risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities and address potential data loss risks proactively.

Assess the risk from the DLP incident monitoring results and report the monitoring results to the Head of IT Risk (TH) and Head of Risk and Fraud (TH).

Participate in IT risk Assessment of significant project and/or assigned projects

Assess the IT risk from the Project and report the risk assessment results to the Head of IT Risk and Head of Risk and Fraud (TH).

Develop and deliver training programs to educate employees on data protection best practices, DLP policies, and their role in safeguarding sensitive information.

Collaborate with IT, IT Security, DPO, legal, compliance, and other departments to ensure data security requirements are incorporated into business processes and new projects.

Serve as the primary point of contact for all DLP-related matters and provide guidance on data protection challenges.

Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Data 

Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in Data loss prevention, Information Security, Data Analytics with 1-3 years in a leadership role focused on data loss prevention preferred

Hands-on experience with DLP tools and technologies.

Proven experience managing security incidents, risk assessments, and audits.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to explain complex security concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

Problem-solving mindset with the ability to think critically and strategically.

Job_Title: Physical Security & ICT Design Engineer (Data Center)
Full_Description:
Role Description:

We are seeking an experienced Physical Security & ICT Design Engineer to join our mission-critical Data Center design team. The ideal candidate will lead end-to-end design and coordination of ICT infrastructure and physical security systems for greenfield and brownfield Data Center projects. This includes structured cabling, telecom/network infrastructure, MMR/CR/MDA design, hyperscale compliance, system integration, and commissioning. The role requires strong knowledge of industry standards (TIA-942, ISO/IEC 11801, ISO/IEC 22237, ISO/IEC 27001, SBD) and collaboration with internal teams, clients, architects, MEP consultants, carriers, and vendors.

Key Responsibilities

ICT System Design & Development

Lead full lifecycle ICT design for Data Centers, covering structured cabling (copper/fiber), MMR/Carrier Rooms, Meet-me Rooms, MDAs, tenant rooms/racks, OOB/OA/Enterprise networks, fiber backbone (ISP/OSP), containment systems (tray/ladder/racks), conduits, and Wi-Fi/IoT planning.

Produce ICT high-level and detailed designs: SLDs, schematics, risers, rack elevations, cable schedules, BoM/BoQ, technical specs, and tender documents.

Design end-to-end telecom infrastructure including fiber entry pathways, redundant routing, and cross-connect architectures.

Ensure compliance with TIA-942, ISO/IEC 11801, ISO/IEC 22237, Uptime Tier Guidelines, and hyperscaler design guides (AWS, Meta, Google, Microsoft).

Physical Security System Design

Lead design of Access Control, Intrusion Detection, CCTV/Video Surveillance, PIDS, mantraps/secure portals, and control room/GSOC layouts.

Select compliant hardware: cameras, VMS, NDAA-compliant devices, biometric/OSDP card readers, NVRs/servers/storage, turnstiles, electric locks, and egress devices.

Conduct security risk assessments, CPTED reviews, and Security by Design evaluations.

Ensure alignment with local codes, DC operator standards, and international security guidelines.

Project Management & Coordination

Coordinate with architects, MEP, hyperscale/tenant teams, carriers/ISPs, ICT/security vendors, GCs, and subcontractors.

Review shop drawings, material submissions, RFIs, and provide technical guidance throughout the lifecycle.

Track schedules, manage deliverables, and prepare reports, presentations, and client-facing documentation.

Commissioning & Testing

Develop commissioning plans and test scripts for structured cabling, fiber/copper testing (Tier 1/Tier 2), security FAT/SAT, and integrated security systems.

Lead/support commissioning: validate installations, conduct inspections, prepare punch lists, acceptance tests, coordinate with carriers/integrators, and complete test reports, as-builts, and handover packages.

Documentation & Compliance

Produce technical designs, method statements, risk assessments, commissioning reports, as-builts, and O&M manuals.

Ensure compliance with local regulations, international ICT/security standards, and client/corporate quality requirements.

Qualifications & Technical Requirements

Degree in Engineering, ICT, Security, Telecommunications, or related field (preferred).

Minimum 3 years’ experience in ICT and security system design/engineering/commissioning for critical infrastructure (Data Centers preferred).

Proven experience designing/integrating:

Access Control Systems

Intrusion Detection Systems

CCTV/Video Surveillance

Perimeter Detection & Monitoring

Control Room Operations

Structured Cabling (Cat6A, OS2, MPO/MTP)

Cross-connect/patching architectures

Redundant fiber routing, cable management, containment

Carrier/ISP fiber delivery methods

Network room environmental requirements (power/cooling/space)

Proficiency with design tools (AutoCAD, Revit/BIM, Visio), Bluebeam, MS Project.

Preferred certifications: RCDD (BICSI) or equivalent, PSP/CPP, Security by Design credentials, CDCP/CDCS/DCDC.

Strong understanding of local security codes, building regulations, ISO 27001, TIA-942, and low-voltage/structured cabling fundamentals.

Knowledge of network basics (Layer 1/2, VLANs, OOB networks) is advantageous.

Experience collaborating with architects, MEP consultants, telecom/ICT teams.

Strong English communication skills and project management proficiency (MS Project, Trello, etc.).

Preferences

Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and manage multiple projects.

Excellent attention to detail, problem-solving skills, and stakeholder management.

Strong analytical and proactive approach.

Reliable, professional, and able to maintain confidentiality.

Team player with leadership capabilities.

Able to thrive under tight deadlines in fast-paced environments.

Willing to travel for site visits and inspections.

Previous Data Center ICT/security design and commissioning experience.

Experience with international DC standards (TIA-942, ISO/IEC 27001, Uptime Tier Guidelines).

Job_Title: Data Analyst - Operation Fraud and Loss Prevention
Full_Description:
1. Data Analysis & Reporting

Collect, clean, and consolidate stock audit data from multiple systems (POS, inventory system, and audit reports).

Develop dashboards and regular reports showing key metrics such as shrinkage %, top loss SKUs, and high-risk stores.

Perform variance analysis between system stock vs. physical count to detect irregularities and highlight areas of concern.

Identify trends and patterns related to store operations, product categories, or audit results.

2. Strategic Insight & Stakeholder Linkage

Understand the business context behind data — how stock variance impacts finance, operations, and fraud prevention.

Translate analytical results into strategic insights that help stakeholders make decisions (e.g., Area Managers, Operations, Finance, and Loss Prevention teams).

Map data relationships between systems and functions (sales, purchasing, stock transfer, promotion, write-off) to ensure holistic analysis.

Provide data-based recommendations that align with company loss reduction goals (e.g., reduce stock loss to 0.2% of sales).

Anticipate how findings can influence policy, store SOPs, and audit focus.

3. Root Cause Investigation

Collaborate with audit and store teams to identify root causes of discrepancies (process gaps, theft, system errors).

Support fraud investigations with data evidence such as transaction trails, timestamps, and behavior patterns.

Apply analytical techniques (correlation, trend analysis, anomaly detection) to uncover hidden causes.

4. Dashboard & Data Visualization

Design and maintain Power BI/Tableau/LookerStudio dashboards to monitor stock loss, count accuracy, and high-risk stores.

Automate recurring reports and build exception alerts for unusual stock variance or suspicious transactions.

Present insights through visuals and summaries that are actionable for non-technical audiences.

5. Process Improvement & Business Impact

Recommend improvements to stock audit procedures and store controls based on data findings.

Track the results of corrective actions and quantify impact on loss reduction.

Contribute to the development of data-driven SOPs for stock counting and reconciliation.

6. Collaboration & Communication

Work closely with IT/Data teams to improve data structure and data flow.

Partner with Finance, Operations, and Loss Prevention teams to ensure consistent interpretation of data and KPIs.

Communicate findings clearly to management through reports and presentations that link data insights to business strategies.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Accounting, or related field.

2–4 years of experience in data analysis, preferably in retail, audit, or inventory management.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a strategic mindset.

Proficiency in Excel, visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau), SQL (preferable)

Understanding of data relationships across retail processes (sales, inventory, transfer, shrinkage).

Ability to translate data into actionable insights for multiple stakeholders.

Strong communication and storytelling skills.

5. ประสานงานกับฝ่ายงานภายในเพื่อนิยามเงื่อนไขและความต้องการของผู้ใช้งานระบบ   

6. รายงานและติดตามผลการแก้จุดบกพร่องของระบบในผลิตภัณฑ์ของบริษัท   

7. รายงานและติดตามการวัดผลคุณภาพของระบบในผลิตภัณฑ์ของบริษัท   

8. ศึกษาและปรับปรุงเทคนิคการทดสอบระบบและการประกันคุณภาพซอฟต์แวร์ให้ทันสมัยอยู่เสมอ   

9. ร่วมวางแผนงานกับผู้จัดการโครงการ และผู้จัดการทั่วไป เพื่อให้มีความสอดคล้องการความต้องการของลูกค้า รวมถึงเข้าร่วมการประชุม การหารือ กับลูกค้า อันเกี่ยวเนื่องกับโครงการหรือผลิตภัณฑ์ของบริษัทที่ได้รับผิดชอบหรือได้รับมอบหมาย 

คุณสมบัติ

1. วุฒิการศึกษาปริญญาตรี สาขาวิทยาการคอมพิวเตอร์, เทคโนโลยีสารสนเทศ (IT) หรือสาขาที่เกี่ยวข้อง หรือมีประสบการณ์เทียบเท่า 

2. มีประสบการณ์ 5 ปีขึ้นไป ในบทบาทการประกันคุณภาพซอฟต์แวร์ (Software Quality Assurance) หรือตำแหน่งนักทดสอบซอฟต์แวร์ (Testing) 

3. มีความเข้าใจอย่างดี เกี่ยวกับระเบียบวิธี, เครื่องมือ และกระบวนการของงานประกันคุณภาพ (QA) 

4. มีประสบการณ์ ในการเขียนแผนการทดสอบ (Test Plans) และกรณีทดสอบ (Test Cases) ที่ชัดเจนและครอบคลุม 

5. มีประสบการณ์ ในการใช้งานซอฟต์แวร์สำหรับติดตามข้อบกพร่อง (Bug Tracking) (เช่น Jira) 

6. มีความใส่ใจในรายละเอียดที่ยอดเยี่ยม และมีความมุ่งมั่นในคุณภาพของงาน 

7. หากมีประสบการณ์ ในการใช้เฟรมเวิร์กสำหรับทดสอบอัตโนมัติ (Test Automation Frameworks) (เช่น Selenium, Cypress, Pytest) จะได้รับการพิจารณาเป็นพิเศษ

About Sony Device Technology (Thailand)

Sony Device Technology (Thailand) Co., Ltd. (SDT) serves as a core production and testing facility for Sony’s semiconductor business. Our facility in Bang Kadi Industrial Park specializes in the assembly and testing of Image Sensors and microscale semiconductor devices integrated into automotive safety and imaging systems.




Job Summary

As a Tester Engineer, you are responsible for maintaining and optimizing the high-precision semiconductor testing environment. Your role involves managing the technical lifecycle of test hardware, performing deep-dive circuit analysis to resolve failures, and utilizing data automation to drive yield improvements. You will ensure all testing processes comply with automotive-grade quality standards and support seamless product transitions from NPI to mass production.




Key Responsibilities

Manage the end-to-end technical setup, calibration, and hands-on maintenance of Automatic Test Equipment (ATE), including load boards, probers, and specialized fixtures. Perform advanced circuit analysis at the component level to diagnose complex hardware failures and electrical signal integrity issues. Utilize multimeters, oscilloscopes, and logic analyzers to execute hardware repairs and ensure 100% equipment readiness for New Product Introduction (NPI) and sustained mass production.

Analyze large-scale production datasets to identify yield detractors, cycle time bottlenecks, and manufacturing inefficiencies. Develop and implement Excel Macros (VBA) and custom automation scripts to streamline data processing, validate test result integrity, and automate technical reporting. Lead Root Cause Analysis (RCA) investigations into non-conforming (NG) defects using 8D or Why-Why methodologies to deploy permanent technical solutions and improve overall test stability.

Standardize technical documentation, including Operating Procedures (SOPs) and IC qualification protocols, ensuring strict adherence to automotive quality standards (IATF 16949). Partner with Design, Production, and Quality teams to evaluate "Design for Testability" (DFT) based on circuit performance data. Manage test correlation activities across multiple equipment platforms to ensure consistent accuracy and reliability across all production lots.




Qualifications & Competencies

Minimum Qualifications:

Bachelor’s Degree in Electronics Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical discipline.

0 – 3 years of experience in test engineering, equipment maintenance, or electronics manufacturing.

Demonstrated ability in circuit analysis and hardware diagnostics; skilled in using Multimeters, Oscilloscopes, and Logic Analyzers for troubleshooting.

High proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including the ability to develop and debug Macros and VBA scripts for data automation and analysis.

Strong logical and systematic problem-solving skills with experience in technical root cause analysis.




Preferred Qualifications:

Direct experience in semiconductor testing or electronics manufacturing.

Proficiency with statistical analysis software (e.g., JMP, Minitab) or data visualization tools.

Knowledge of automotive quality management systems (IATF 16949) and ESD control protocols in a production environment.

Relevant internship or senior project experience related to IC testing or automated systems.




Language & Professional Skills:

Ability to read and write technical documentation and reports in English. TOEIC 600+ is preferred.

Ability to communicate technical findings clearly to cross-functional teams in a fast-paced environment.

Strong attention to technical detail and disciplined adherence to safety and quality protocols.




Working Conditions

Ability to work in a Cleanroom environment, necessitating the use of a full cleanroom suit.

Ability to support on-call duties as required by the production schedule to ensure minimal downtime.

Testing Execution & Methodologies

Conduct a variety of testing methodologies including functional, non-functional, integration, system, and user acceptance testing (UAT)

Apply black box testing to validate application behavior from a user perspective, and white box testing when needed to assist developers in internal validation

Automation & Efficiency

Design and implement automated test suites to improve regression test coverage and reduce manual testing time

Integrate automated tests with CI/CD pipelines to ensure fast and reliable feedback during development

Contribute to building a robust QA automation framework using modern testing tools

Process Improvement & Quality Standards

Continuously evaluate and improve QA processes, aligning with team SOPs and evolving engineering workflows

Define and track QA-related KPIs (e.g., bug escape rate, test coverage, automation percentage) to ensure measurable quality improvement

Actively participate in team retrospectives and sprint reviews to advocate for quality at every stage

Reporting & Communication

Report bugs and potential issues clearly and effectively with detailed reproduction steps and impact assessments

Communicate test results and provide clear, actionable feedback to developers, product owners, and other stakeholders

Maintain well-structured test case documentation and testing guides for current and future team members

Learning & Adaptation

Stay current with QA trends, tools, and technologies (e.g., Playwright, Cypress, Postman, Allure, etc.)
